phase1_specify.summary: Phase 1 Specify generated the PRSG-013 feature spec and requirements quality checklist.

outcomes: Durable marker plan lives as top-level pr_marker_plan state, sibling to layer_plan and multi_pr_emission.
Stable marker IDs are foundation, us<N>, us<N>-part<M>, and full-spec for hazard collapse; markers[] order plus one-based review_order is authoritative.
Each marker records source boundary, task IDs, folded Polish task IDs, declared files/tests, reviewability, hazards, subdivision, implementation checkpoint, and emission mapping.
Foundation is optional, one marker is created per user story, oversized stories may subdivide, and small Polish folds into the nearest non-Polish marker.
Resume validates a source_fingerprint and replaces stale marker plans while preserving child evidence only for unchanged marker IDs.

Safe task clusters are contiguous tasks inside one user story with no dependency edge crossing the boundary, complete declared files/tests, no shared mutation or hazard signal, and preserved task order.
Oversized stories without safe internal boundaries keep the original us<N> marker, continue, and record structured warnings.
Hazard collapse triggers only when Atomicity Route has route == single-atomic-PR or releasable == false; one-navigable-PR with releasable true is not a hazard-collapse trigger.
Implementation still follows original marker order under hazard collapse, then emits one full-spec PR mapped back to original marker IDs.
Marker warnings use structured code/severity/message/source/details evidence and render readable strings into PR packet warnings.
clarify_results.consensus: Round 1 codebase/spec consensus agreed on the hazard-collapse predicate.

outcomes: All parseable reviewability results feed marker planning; only unusable evidence is a correctness stop.
Post-G5 reviewability-gate tasks exit 1 with valid status=block JSON is captured as marker input and does not stop implementation.
Final full-diff size block with a current valid pr_marker_plan returns marker_split, exits successfully, and passes evidence into marker-based PR emission.
Missing, stale, malformed, or fingerprint-mismatched marker plans at final emission remain correctness stops.
Each marker emission mapping carries final-backstop status, metrics, evidence path, and structured warning objects.
clarify_results.consensus: Round 1 codebase/spec consensus agreed on marker_split proceed behavior.

consensus_resolution_log.outcome: Hazard collapse triggers only for route == single-atomic-PR or releasable == false; one-navigable-PR with releasable true does not trigger hazard collapse by itself.

When the final backstop sees a full-diff size block and a current valid pr_marker_plan exists, it returns marker_split, exits 0, and passes evidence to marker-based PR emission. Invalid marker state remains a correctness stop.
